opportunities.event_name: Federal Reserve FOMC Meeting Minutes Release
opportunities.rationale: First minutes release under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh; markets expect clues on rate-cut path (1 cut in 2026 vs 2 priced in futures). VIX closed 18.01 May 19 (Yahoo Finance) — above the 18 trending-bullish threshold; hawkish surprise could spike vol toward 20-22. S&P 500 at 7,410 / SPY $733.73, QQQ $702.36. 10Y yield 4.667% (MarketWatch May 19) at multi-year highs — any dovish pivot is a catalyst for TLT and GLD. Invalidated if minutes confirm unchanged data-dependent stance with no new guidance.

opportunities.company_name: S&P Global US Flash PMIs — May 2026
opportunities.event_name: S&P Global Flash Manufacturing & Services PMI (May 2026)
opportunities.rationale: April ISM Manufacturing held at 52.7 (4th consecutive expansionary month). May Flash PMIs are the first real-time read on whether momentum continues. XLB at $49.04 (below both MAs) and NUE at $221.73 (below 20d MA) indicate materials/industrials are already pricing in risk. With S&P above all MAs at 7,410, a miss below 50 could trigger sector rotation out of cyclicals. A beat above 52.7 would reconfirm XLI/XLB longs.

opportunities.company_name: US Housing Starts & Building Permits — April 2026
opportunities.event_name: US Housing Starts and Building Permits (April 2026 data)
opportunities.rationale: Housing data with Fed on hold and mortgage rates elevated (30Y ~7%). XHB at $96.41 close May 19 (SSGA/MarketWatch) — down from $103 highs in Feb 2026. DHI at $135.27 (Perplexity Finance May 19), LEN at $82.64 (Yahoo Finance May 19) — both showing weakness. Weak print could pressure homebuilders further. HD already reported cautiously. Strong print = short-term relief for XHB.

opportunities.company_name: US Initial Jobless Claims — Week ending May 17
opportunities.event_name: US Initial Jobless Claims Weekly Report
opportunities.rationale: Weekly jobless claims are the most current labour market signal ahead of June 6 NFP. 10Y yield at 4.667% shows market pricing in no near-term Fed cuts. Elevated claims would validate rate-cut hopes; low claims reinforce no-cut stance. VIX at 18.01 means claims surprise can move markets. Normal 210k-240k range = no reaction. Invalidated if reading remains in normal range.

WTI at $103.20 May 19 close (investing.com historical data); intraday touched near $105 per riskreversal.com commentary. Oil above $100 sustained by Middle East risk premium and OPEC+ discipline. Energy sector leading all sectors weekly. XLE at $61.29, XOM at $162.62 — both above 20d MAs confirming energy strength. 50d MA for WTI ~$94-96. Invalidated below $94 (structural demand break).
opportunities.entry_trigger: Hold above $100 with XLE above 20d MA $59; scale out at $110-115

opportunities.rationale: GLD closed $413.77 May 19 (investing.com historical). 52-week high $509.70 (Jan 2026). Pulled back ~19% from all-time highs. Elevated safe-haven demand despite equity greed (CNN F&G 62.66). 10Y yield at 4.667% is headwind but gold holding. FOMC minutes May 20 is the key catalyst — dovish tone = GLD upside. SSGA NAV data shows $415.75 as of May 15. Stop below $398 GLD if confirmed hawkish pivot.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on or after FOMC minutes if dovish signal; or hold above $408 GLD (support zone)

opportunities.company_name: ECB June Meeting — Rate Path Signal
opportunities.rationale: ECB deposit rate at 2.00% after March 19 cut. June meeting may confirm further easing given subdued EU inflation. EUR/USD elevated; Fed-ECB divergence widening if Fed stays on hold at 3.50-3.75% while ECB cuts. EURUSD short could be viable if ECB cuts while Fed stays. 10Y US yield at 4.667% reinforces USD strength. Invalidated if ECB signals pause.

opportunities.event_name: US Non-Farm Payrolls and Unemployment Rate (May 2026)
opportunities.rationale: Next NFP (May data) on June 6. Under new Fed Chair Warsh with 1 cut projected for 2026, a weak jobs report could front-run that cut and spark broad risk rally. S&P at 7,410 / SPY $733.73 near ATH; VIX at 18.01. Outsized reaction likely if miss. LOW conviction — event too far out for precise positioning. Watch for jobless claims drift toward 250k+ as leading indicator. Invalidated if macro data resets expectations between now and June 6.
